results.title: How We Taught AI Agents to Navigate Company Data Like a Filesystem<!-- --> | Dust Blog
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > **Crafting a structure to navigate**
Content:
Fortunately, all of this work had already been done in the context of the migration to the so-called content nodes architecture . Only, we had no idea back then that this hierarchy would have a use beyond allowing users to select subsets of their knowledge base in the Agent Builder.
 ... 
Section Title: How We Taught AI Agents to Navigate Company Data Like a Filesystem > ... > **Context window issues**
Content:
One interesting challenge came with `cat` . In Unix, it dumps the entire file. But AI agents have context windows, which are hard limits on how much text they can process. A naive implementation would have agents trying to read massive files and immediately failing.
We added `limit` and `offset` parameters: the agent chooses to see a certain number of lines starting from a certain line number.
`cat: { nodeId: string, offset?: number, // Start position limit?: number, // Max characters grep?: string // Filter lines }`
This lets agents read documents in chunks, jump to specific sections, and filter content, all without exploding their context windows. This lets agents handle arbitrarily large documents.
 ... 
Section Title: How We Taught AI Agents to Navigate Company Data Like a Filesystem > Related Posts
Content:

results.title: Enterprise knowledge management: A comprehensive guide 2024
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > How do Gen AI products like Glean enhance knowledge management in enterprises?
Content:
Glean revolutionizes knowledge management for modern teams by consolidating all company knowledge into one accessible platform. Whether it's finding quick answers to frequently asked questions, organizing collections of links and documents, or creating memorable go-links for common resources, Glean streamlines the process for easy discovery and navigation.
**Answers:** Say goodbye to redundant answers with Glean's intelligent search ranking, which surfaces the most relevant responses to queries, even for variations of questions. Integration with Slack allows for seamless saving and automatic surfacing of answers from conversations. Admin controls ensure that specific answers are visible only to designated departments, maintaining information security and relevance.
 ...

results.title: AI agents examples with Dust: Use cases across different teams<!-- --> | Dust Blog
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > **1. Brevo's sales agent that personalizes outreach**
Content:
**Customer referral finder:** Surfaces relevant customer examples for social proof before sales calls
**Personalized email generator:** Creates multi-variant email sequences based on prospect context and company data
**Landing page personalization agent:** Generates custom marketing plans for inbound leads in real time
**Result:** 80% time reduction on email personalization research, bringing BDR work down from 30+ minutes per prospect to just minutes. The team has executed 2,500+ production actions through their Supabase-connected agents with zero engineering tickets filed.
**💡** [**Read the full Brevo story →**](https://dust.tt/customers/brevo-gtm-team-workflows-faster-email-personalization)
 ... 
Section Title: ... > **4. Persona's knowledge agents across engineering and sales**
Content:
**PersonaEngineer (orchestrator):** Routes questions to the right sub-agent
**DDDEngineer:** Searches GitHub codebases
**InfrastructureEngineer:** Handles production changes and incidents
**DataQueryExpert:** Queries data warehouses
**PersonaHelpCenter:** Searches technical documentation
**PersonaGlossary:** Resolves internal terminologies
**PeopleNerd:** Searches the employee directory
The success of PersonaEngineer inspired other teams to build their own standalone agents:
**RFPNerd:** Answers RFP questions using past proposals and company data
**SRDNerd:** Generates Solution Requirement Documents automatically from call transcripts
**Result:** Within six months, Persona hit 80%+ company-wide adoption, with 85% of Sales and Post-Sales teams actively using agents.
 ... 
Section Title: AI agents examples with Dust: Use cases across different teams > **📌 TL;DR** > **More about Dust**
Content:
The companies in this article are using Dust to deploy AI agents at scale. Dust is an operating system for AI agents — a platform that lets teams deploy, orchestrate, and govern specialized agents connected to their company's knowledge and tools.
You connect the tools your team already uses: Notion, Google Drive, Slack, Salesforce, GitHub, Zendesk, and dozens more. Then you write instructions for what the agent should do.
Agents can answer questions, research across systems, generate content, or query databases in plain language. The process of [building an AI agent](https://dust.tt/blog/how-to-build-an-ai-agent) is accessible and requires no coding experience.
Teams deploy agents where work happens: in Slack, inside support tickets through Zendesk, or via a browser extension that works anywhere on the web.

**Multi-step execution:** They can break down complex tasks and execute them across multiple tools
**Tool integration:** Direct connections to your existing systems (Slack, Notion, Salesforce, etc.)
**Knowledge base access:** Real-time access to your company's documentation and data
**Orchestration:** Multiple agents can collaborate on complex workflows
How quickly can we get started with Dust?
Most teams are up and running within minutes. Our no-code builder means you can create your first custom agent without any technical expertise. Connect your data sources, configure your agent's instructions, and you're ready to go. For enterprise deployments, we offer dedicated onboarding support.

results.title: Top Glean alternatives for AI-powered enterprise search (2026)<!-- --> | Dust Blog
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > **1. Dust - #1 alternative to Glean for enterprise search, powered by AI agents**
Content:
**What makes Dust's search better?** Dust doesn't just find documents. It's powered by [semantic search](https://docs.dust.tt/docs/understanding-retrieval-augmented-generation-rag-and-the-search-method-in-dust) that reads them, understands what you're looking for, and gives you actual answers. Think of it as the difference between someone handing you a stack of papers versus someone who tells you exactly what you need to know.
Plus, Dust is an agentic first platform that goes further. It's an AI agent operating system that helps you complete the work those documents enable. Traditional enterprise search stops at retrieval. Dust agents generate reports, analyze data, create content, and solve problems based on your company's knowledge. Search becomes the starting point for getting work done, not the end goal.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > **What are the best Glean alternatives?**
Content:
Dust is an excellent solution for enterprise search, but it does way more than that. With AI agents that automate workflows, generate documents, and take action on information, Dust transforms how teams work with knowledge beyond just finding it.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > **What is Dust and how is it different?**
Content:
Dust combines enterprise search with AI agents that can automate tasks, generate documents, and take action on information, not just find it.
